ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANTS DAVOL, INC.'S AND C.R. BARD, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS C.R. BARD, INC. AND DAVOL, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. THE QUESTION OF WHETHER A PLAINTIFF WHO HAS FAILED TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION SHOULD BE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND HIS OR HER COMPLAINT IS ENTRUSTED TO THE DISCRETION OF THE TRIAL COURT. (CCP 472A(C).) THE COURT SUSTAINS DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE AMENDED FORM COMPLAINT, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH THE OPPORTUNITY TO STATE SPECIFICALLY THE GROUNDS UPON WHICH HE ASSERTS HIS STRICT PRODUCTS LIABILITY CLAIM AGAINST C.R. BARD, INC. AND TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT SUPPORT A STRICT PRODUCTS LIABILITY CLAIM BASED ON A THEORY OF DEFECTIVE MANUFACTURING OR ON THE THEORY THAT THE DEFENDANT DISTRIBUTED THE PRODUCT WITHOUT ADEQUATE WARNINGS. THE DEMURRER IS ALSO SUSTAINED, WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND, TO PROVIDE PLAINTIFF WITH AN OPPORTUNITY TO ALLEGE FACTS THAT C.R. BARD, INC. MADE AN EXPRESS WARRANTY TO PLAINTIFF REGARDING THE SAFETY OF THE 3DMAX SURGICAL MESH IMPLANT AND THAT THE EXPRESS WARRANTY FORMED THE BASIS OF THE BARGAIN. (HOUSTON V. MEDTRONIC, INC. (2013) 957 F.SUPP.2D 1166, 1181.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302/EHG

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-11-2015 Continued To Mar-09-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANTS C.R. BARD, INC. AND DAVOL, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained, with ten days leave to amend. The question of whether a plaintiff who has failed to state a cause of action should be given leave to amend his or her complaint is entrusted to the discretion of the trial court. (CCP 472a(c).) The court sustains Defendants' demurrer to the amended form complaint, with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with the opportunity to state specifically the grounds upon which he asserts his strict products liability claim against C.R. Bard, Inc. and to allege facts that support a strict products liability claim based on a theory of defective manufacturing or on the theory that the defendant distributed the product without adequate warnings. The demurrer is also sustained, with ten days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiff with an opportunity to allege facts that C.R. Bard, Inc. made an express warranty to Plaintiff regarding the safety of the 3DMax surgical mesh implant and that the express warranty formed the basis of the bargain. (Houston v. Medtronic, Inc. (2013) 957 F.Supp.2d 1166, 1181.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg
